author: David Reed
summary: Phase H1 first extraction. Decision Support and Medical Director system prompts moved from f-string constants in templates.py to file-level mount points at src/pacca/agents/<agent>/system_prompt.md. A Jinja2 loader assembles prompts at runtime from the .md file plus the shared components in templates.py. Byte-identity with pre-H1 output verified character-by-character before any runtime change. Full test suite (120 tests) passes unchanged. Three findings recorded, deferred to separate commits.

changes.description: Extract Decision Support and Medical Director system prompts to file-level mount points; add Jinja2 loader; wire decision.py to use the loader; declare jinja2 (introduced by the loader) and reconcile python-jose/bcrypt (missing from pyproject.toml but present in requirements.txt) in pyproject.toml's dependencies.
changes.failure_pattern: No failure pattern targeted. This is a structural commit that establishes the file-level decoupling Phases H2 and H3 require. The H1 success criterion is byte-identical prompt output, not a behavioral gain.
changes.root_cause: Pre-H1, agent prompts lived as Python f-string constants inside prompts/templates.py, mixed with Python module-level interpolation logic. Edits to prompts produced diffs that mixed prompt-text changes with module-rendering changes. Phases H2 (Institutional Memory Layer) and H3 (Cross-Step Middleware Tier) require one-file-per-component diffs to attribute behavioral gains correctly; that attribution is impossible without H1 first.

evidence.note: Custom verification script comparing loader output to f-string output character-by-character. Both DecisionSupportAgent and MedicalDirectorAgent prompts produced byte-identical output after a one-character fix to the Decision Support .md file (a missing blank line between the role section and the safety guidelines block). Documented as part of the H1 verification gate.

All 18 prompt-engineering tests pass unchanged. Tests that directly compare DECISION_AGENT_SYSTEM string content continue to pass because that constant remains canonical in templates.py; the loader's Jinja2 substitution reproduces it from the file.
120 of 120 collectable tests pass. No regressions introduced.

evidence.source: GitHub Actions CI on PR for harness/iter-1
evidence.note: Initial PR-CI run revealed (a) jinja2 was a new runtime dependency introduced by the loader and not declared, and (b) python-jose was already missing from pyproject.toml independently. Both reconciled in this commit. The deferral of python-jose/bcrypt to a separate commit was correct in principle but the CI run made the dependency surface visible enough that bundling them into chg-1 became the cleaner outcome.

The iter-0 schema's files-path pattern restricted entries to src/pacca/, harness/, docs/, and tests/. The iter-1 PR-CI cycle revealed this was too strict: real harness changes sometimes touch root-level config (pyproject.toml here). The pattern was broadened to include repo-root config files (pyproject.toml, requirements*.txt, setup.py/cfg, Dockerfile, .gitignore, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE, Makefile) and CI workflows under .github/. Also generalized src/pacca/ to src/ since the project-specific prefix was unnecessary. This is the methodology working as designed: the schema constraints themselves age into discoveries that age into improvements.

Dead code observed during chg-1: defines DecisionSupportAgent at line 52, imported by no module (orchestrator and tests use decision.py's DecisionAgent). Deletion queued as chg-2. Recorded as evidence because the schema has no deferred-work field; the prose record is in DECISIONS.md.

Observed during chg-1: houses both Tier 1 DecisionAgent and Tier 2 MedicalDirectorAgent in one module. Both prompts extracted to separate .md mount points without restructuring classes. A class-level split, if later desired, would be its own iteration.

changes.why_this_component: The system prompt is the constraint level being decoupled. Tool descriptions, tool implementations, middleware, and other component types remain unchanged in this commit; their extraction is queued for future iterations. The choice to extract only system prompts in chg-1 keeps the commit scope narrow and the verification gate simple (byte-identity check on rendered prompt strings).
changes.rollback_plan: git revert <sha>. The orphaned decision_agent.py was not modified. templates.py was not modified. Reverting decision.py and removing the new directories restores the pre-H1 state exactly. PROMPT_REGISTRY versions remain unchanged across the refactor, so audit logs are reconcilable.
